title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What is a term insurance plan?
A term insurance plan offers pure protection. It is a life insurance product that provides you with a life cover for a specified period in return for regular and periodic payments known as premiums. In the event of your death during the term of the plan, the life insurance company would provide the nominees or beneficiaries of the policy with a death benefit, subject to policy terms and conditions.
The death benefit is a certain predetermined sum of money that is paid out by the insurer, which the beneficiaries can make use of as they see fit. This way, you can help safeguard your family’s future even when you are no longer alive.
The premium of term insurance plans may be comparatively lower than other types of life insurance plans. This makes them one of the most cost-effective options for an individual to secure the financial future of their family.
Benefits of term insurance
Now that you are aware of the differences between term insurance vs health insurance, here is a quick look at some of the term insurance benefits that you get to enjoy –
1. They are cost-effective
The premium that you are required to pay for a good term insurance plan is lower when compared to other insurance products. For instance, you can even get a term insurance plan for a premium that is as low as Rs. 500 per month subject to policy terms & conditions.
2. They are very flexible
Depending on the plan you opt for, you can choose to receive the benefits of a term insurance policy either as a lump sum or on a monthly basis. If you choose to receive the benefits on a monthly basis, you may further have the option to choose to receive a fixed-sum each month or a monthly pay-out increasing yearly.
3. They come with add-on riders
You can enhance the benefits offered by a term insurance policy by opting to add on certain riders at a nominal extra cost. For instance, the critical illness rider provides you with an additional lump sum benefit if you are diagnosed with any one of the diseases mentioned in the rider document. This benefit is paid over and above the base policy’s sum assured amount.
4. They come with several tax benefits
One of the most important term insurance benefits is that it comes with several tax benefits. According to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (the Act), the premiums that you pay towards a term insurance plan are eligible to be deducted from your total income to the tune of Rs. 1.5 lakh each financial year, under old tax regime. That is not all, the death benefit that your nominees or your beneficiaries receive is also completely exempt from taxation under section 10(10D) of the Act. The above deductions and exemptions are subject to the provisions contained in the Act.
Types of Term Insurance Policies
Term insurance is a type of insurance that pays money to your family if something happens to you. It is simple and affordable. There are different types of term insurance plans to match your needs.
Standard Term Insurance Plan
In this plan, the premium stays the same throughout. If the life assured passes away during the policy period, the nominee gets the full sum assured.
Return of Premium Term Insurance
This is a special type of term insurance policy. If the policyholder survives the term, all the premiums paid are returned. If they pass away during the term, the sum assured is paid to the nominee.
Increasing Term Insurance
This type of term insurance policy gives a higher sum assured each year. It helps deal with inflation. The premium may also increase a little every year.
Decreasing Term Insurance
In this plan, the sum assured reduces every year. It is suitable if your financial needs go down over time. Premiums usually stay the same throughout the policy.
Term Insurance with Riders and Options
You can add extra benefits to your term plan, like accident cover, critical illness cover, or waiver of premium. These are called riders. They help increase protection by paying a little more.
How does term insurance with a critical illness rider work?
As you have already seen above, the critical illness benefit rider is an add-on rider that you can include over and above your base term insurance policy at a nominal extra cost.
This rider can enhance the coverage of your term insurance policy by providing you with an additional sum assured in the event where you get diagnosed with any one of the critical illnesses or diseases specified in the rider document. The additional rider sum assured amount that you receive could be used to cover your treatment costs for the disease.
For instance, let us assume that you opt for a term insurance plan with a death benefit of Rs. 50 lakhs for a period of 20 years. The premium that you are required to pay is around Rs. 3,000 per year.
Now, at the time of purchasing the policy, let’s assume that you also opt for the critical illness rider for the same tenure of 20 years. The rider premium is set at Rs. 1,000 per year. The rider sum assured that you would receive upon diagnosis of any one of the diseases mentioned in the rider document is set at Rs. 15 lakhs.
In the event where you first are diagnosed with a disease mentioned in the policy document, the insurer would be obligated to pay your Rs. 15 lakhs as a lump sum amount. You can then use this amount towards your hospitalization and treatment expenses. In the event where you face an untimely demise due to this disease, the insurer would pay the death benefit of Rs. 50 lakhs as per the base policy to your nominees or beneficiaries.
Conclusion
Both term insurance and health insurance are two very essential insurance products that you, as an individual, should consider investing in. This way, you can get coverage and financial protection from the situations that life throws your way.
FAQs
Q1. What is better, health insurance or term insurance?
Both are important and serve different purposes. Health insurance pays for your hospital bills when you are sick or injured. Whereas, a term insurance policy helps your family financially if you are no longer around. It depends on your needs. If you want to cover hospital expenses, buy health insurance. If you want your family to be financially secure after your death, choose a term plan. Most people prefer to have both to stay fully protected.
Q2. Which disease is not covered in term insurance?
Term insurance is not a health plan, so it does not cover diseases or hospital costs. It only pays money to your family if you pass away during the policy term. Some term insurance plans may have riders, like critical illness, but they only cover certain listed illnesses. Always read the term insurance policy document carefully to know what is excluded.
Q3. How many years is best for term insurance?
The best tenure for term insurance depends on your age, job, and family needs. If you're in your 20s or 30s, you can choose between a 30 to 40-year term. This helps keep premiums low and provides longer protection for your family. The policy should cover the years until your family becomes financially independent. A longer term means you stay protected for more years without needing to renew the plan.
Q4. What happens to term insurance after maturity?
If the term insurance policy reaches the end of its term and you are alive, the policy ends. You don’t get any money back unless it is a return-of-premium plan. In regular plans, there is no benefit to maturity. This is why term insurance is affordable. It offers pure protection. The main goal is to support your family if something happens to you during the policy term.
Q5. Which term insurance is best?
The best term insurance policy is the one that meets your needs. Look for plans that offer enough coverage, affordable premiums, and options like riders for extra protection. A good term insurance policy should also come from a company with a strong claim settlement ratio. Always check how long the policy lasts, whether it has return options, and if it covers critical illnesses. Choose one that keeps your family secure.
Q6. Does health insurance pay for death?
No, health insurance does not pay for death. It only pays for hospital bills, surgery, and medical treatment. If the person dies, the policy stops, and no money is given to the family. To get money after death, one needs a term insurance policy or life insurance. Health insurance is only for covering medical costs while the person is alive.
Q7. Who can not buy term insurance?
People who are very sick or have serious medical conditions may not be able to buy a term insurance policy. Additionally, individuals without income or legal identification proof may not be approved. Insurers may also deny people with risky jobs, bad habits like heavy smoking, or unclear medical records. Every application is checked by the insurer before approval. Age limits may also apply, depending on the plan.
Q8. Can we take 2 term plans?
Yes, you can take more than one term insurance policy. Many people do this to increase their total coverage or to meet different goals. For example, one plan can support your family, and the other can cover a home loan. Make sure that your income supports the total sum assured, as insurance companies verify this before approval. Inform all insurers if you already have another term plan.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. In this plan, the premium stays the same throughout. If the life assured passes away during the policy period, the nominee gets the full sum assured.

If the life assured survives the term, all the premiums paid are returned, subject to policy terms and conditions. If they pass away during the term, the sum assured is paid to the nominee.

This type of term insurance policy the sum assured increases every year . The premium typically remains the same throughout the policy term.

In this plan, the sum assured reduces every year. It is suitable if your financial needs go down over time. Premiums usually stay the same throughout the policy.
How does term insurance with a critical illness rider work?

These riders involve a small additional premium and generally cover only predefined illnesses.

changes.word_original: Look for plans that offer enough coverage, affordable premiums, and options like riders for extra protection.
changes.pdf_final: Look for plans that offer enough coverage, affordable premiums, and add on benefits like riders for extra protection.

changes.word_original: A good term insurance policy should also come from a company with a strong claim settlement ratio.
changes.pdf_final: Also check the claim settlement ratio of the insurer.

changes.word_original: Always check how long the policy lasts, whether it has return options, and if it covers critical illnesses.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Choose one that keeps your family secure.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF
Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Who can not buy term insurance?
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: People who are very sick or have serious medical conditions may not be able to buy a term insurance policy.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Additionally, individuals without income or legal identification proof may not be approved.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Insurers may also deny people with risky jobs, bad habits like heavy smoking, or unclear medical records.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Every application is checked by the insurer before approval.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Age limits may also apply, depending on the plan.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: For example, one plan can support your family, and the other can cover a home loan.
changes.pdf_final: For example, one plan can support your family, and the sum assured from the other plan can help in repayment of home loan etc.

changes.word_original: Make sure that your income supports the total sum assured, as insurance companies verify this before approval.
changes.pdf_final: Inform your insurers if you already have another term plan.

changes.word_original: Inform all insurers if you already have another term plan.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF
